canonical_definition: Across the surveyed literature, dense captioning is most commonly defined as the task of generating rich, descriptive captions for multiple distinct objects or regions within a single image. The prevailing emphasis is on producing descriptions that "go beyond category labels to include contextual details" (Ins-DetCLIP..., Image Textualization...). As one paper notes, this allows a model to "generate a detailed description for objects of interest" rather than just a category name (Ins-DetCLIP...). A more specialized usage appears in the context of biological imaging, where "dense caption generation" is framed as producing a single, comprehensive textual description of an entire image. In this domain-specific application, the goal is to "capture all necessary trait information of a biological specimen" (VLM4Bio...). One paper describes a specific method for eliciting this behavior called "Dense Caption prompting," which involves a two-stage process to prompt a VLM for the detailed caption (VLM4Bio...). Thus, the concept is used to describe both the generation of multiple descriptions for various objects in a general image and the creation of a single, exhaustive description for a specialized image.
papers: Ins-DetCLIP: Aligning Detection Model to Follow Human-Language Instruction
Mitigating Hallucination in Large Multi-Modal Models via Robust Instruction Tuning
Chat-Scene: Bridging 3D Scene and Large Language Models with Object Identifiers
FlexCap: Describe Anything in Images in Controllable Detail
Image Textualization: An Automatic Framework for Generating Rich and Detailed Image Descriptions
SocialGPT: Prompting LLMs for Social Relation Reasoning via Greedy Segment Optimization
VLM4Bio: A Benchmark Dataset to Evaluate Pretrained Vision-Language Models for Trait Discovery from Biological Images

definitions.text: Generating rich, descriptive captions for multiple objects in an image, going beyond category labels to include contextual details.
definitions.paper: Ins-DetCLIP: Aligning Detection Model to Follow Human-Language Instruction

definitions.text: The behavior of generating a detailed, comprehensive textual description of an image, intended to capture all necessary trait information of a biological specimen.
definitions.paper: VLM4Bio: A Benchmark Dataset to Evaluate Pretrained Vision-Language Models for Trait Discovery from Biological Images
